title: Fix caching layers with build args
body:
This fixes a regression introduced in 9b299588c0936376ef9ccd7938f1cef27c831418.
    
ib.Run() is only really needed in the ARG step. On all the other steps, it can cause potentially expensive commands to be executed unecessarily.
    
Closes https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2992
    
Signed-off-by: Antonio Terceiro <antonio.terceiro@linaro.org>
Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
This does not work for the following case
```
FROM ubi8
ARG foo
CMD echo $foo

➜  buildah git:(arg-test) ./bin/buildah bud --layers -t test                    
WARN missing "foo" build argument. Try adding "--build-arg foo=<VALUE>" to the command line 
STEP 1: FROM ubi8
STEP 2: ARG foo
--> 4d29eae14f9
STEP 3: CMD echo $foo
STEP 4: COMMIT test
--> 5ceadb5fe64
5ceadb5fe647658bb7858b1003f45ff5909daa98ae2aeff5dd84f909cdef2c19

➜  buildah git:(arg-test) ./bin/buildah bud --layers -t test1 --build-arg foo=bar
STEP 1: FROM ubi8
STEP 2: ARG foo
--> Using cache 4d29eae14f93b2831fee8b47e0fa8e71518669493cf3bb2797469a0f70a71882
--> 4d29eae14f9
STEP 3: CMD echo $foo
--> Using cache 5ceadb5fe647658bb7858b1003f45ff5909daa98ae2aeff5dd84f909cdef2c19
STEP 4: COMMIT test1
--> 5ceadb5fe64
5ceadb5fe647658bb7858b1003f45ff5909daa98ae2aeff5dd84f909cdef2c19

➜  buildah git:(arg-test) podman run test

➜  buildah git:(arg-test) podman run test1

➜  buildah git:(arg-test)
```

As you can see, even though I passed in `--build-arg` to the command it did not pick up the new ARG value. We need to run `ib.Run()` so that it can execute buildah's in-memory configuration and retrieve the args we want and add it to the Allowed list of arguments that the `Arguments()` function uses to filter against.
We should have a test to catch this case.
We do, the build-arg test is failing. I think I have a fix, just testing it out.
@terceiro https://github.com/containers/buildah/compare/master...umohnani8:arg-cache?expand=1 should fix both issues, can you please incorporate it in your PR and try it out as well
@umohnani8 yes that does the job, thanks!. I have updated this PR accordingly.

Perfect! Thank you for finding the issue :)

LGTM

I think I'm experiencing this issue. I'm running `buildah` inside a container on k8s connected with the gitlab k8s executor. My `buildah` image has the following `buildah` version info:

```
Version:         1.28.0
Go Version:      go1.19.1
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.23.0
Git Commit:      79f41e3235ecc17d711a9aa06ec769234ec95399
Built:           Tue Oct  4 06:34:51 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

`Dockerfile` is:

```
FROM internal-registry/docker-ember-base:v3.5

# only install npm deps if package.json changes
COPY /.npmrc ./
COPY /.bowerrc ./
COPY /bower.json ./
COPY /package.json ./
COPY /yarn.lock ./

# install stuff
RUN \
    npm config set spin false \
    && yarn install --silent --no-progress --non-interactive --pure-lockfile \
    && npm rebuild node-sass \
    && yarn cache clean \
    && bower install --allow-root --config.storage.packages=.bowercache/packages --config.storage.registry=.bowercache/registry \
    && rm -rf .npmcache .bowercache .yarncache tmp;


# pack code into image
COPY / ./

#  --build-arg "CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME=${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}"
ARG CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME
ARG CI_JOB_NAME
ARG CI_EMBER_BUILD

# build
RUN \
    for env in $(echo "${CI_EMBER_BUILD}" | sed "s/,/ /g"); do \
        time ember build --environment=${env} -o build/${env}; \
    done

# test
# running this as a separate step *after* the build above
# for caching purposes...in the case of sporadic failed tests
# at least the build will not need to be redone
#RUN \
#    if [[ "${CI_JOB_NAME}" == "test" ]];then \
#        time ember exam --silent true --environment test --path build/test --test-port 0 --parallel=6 --load-balance --config-file testem-ci.js; \
#    fi;

# removes need for git directory inside container
# to help keep image size down
# SEE: docker-entrypoint.sh in -base image
ARG CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA
ENV EMBER_VERSION_CHECK_VERSION=${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
```

Snippet from `.gitlab-ci.yml`:

```
    # build image
    buildah build \
    -f Dockerfile \
    --no-hosts \
    --pull=always \
    --layers \
    --cache-to   "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/buildah-cache" \
    --cache-from "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/buildah-cache" \
    --build-arg "CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA=${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" \
    --build-arg "CI_JOB_NAME=${CI_JOB_NAME}" \
    --build-arg "CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME=${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}" \
    --build-arg "CI_EMBER_BUILD=${CI_EMBER_BUILD}" \
    $BUILDTAGARGS \
    .
```

Sample output:

```
# run 1
STEP 8/14: COPY / ./
--> Pushing cache internal-registry/buildah-cache:abdb0c45ad58c40e84e87b2bd430004d5ee9b623bea3c1855fa6f7f9a826e476
--> 07b89c96595
STEP 9/14: ARG CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME
--> Pushing cache internal-registry/buildah-cache:7d0379dc3891679a2f296ad4c3c8eb4d3d6d96aee7ed2296ced1ac274304ce7b
--> ef3fb7db575
STEP 10/14: ARG CI_JOB_NAME
--> Pushing cache internal-registry/buildah-cache:a19633b701a0ad5348fd192740478a3e3fffa4f580de22945f3a87154b93f431
--> d499d76f476
STEP 11/14: ARG CI_EMBER_BUILD

--> eab11aa757c

--> c3a44faaa1c
STEP 11/14: ARG CI_EMBER_BUILD
```

For the same pipeline the values of the args should be the same, yet if I rerun the `job` multiple times each step up to `ARG CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME` is cached but then I diverge. Interesting that all of them 'push' this `7d0379dc3891679a2f296ad4c3c8eb4d3d6d96aee7ed2296ced1ac274304ce7b` but it never gets pulled.

Do I have something off or is there some other issue at play?
